items.text: [L-06 — FLOW] Ch11 TextOpen: Rewrite throat-clearing chapter opener — summarizes Ch10 before engaging Ch11's own argument.

dialogues.text:
PROPOSED: The Debunker's Playbook kept sworn testimony from reaching the public for decades. The same secrecy impeded scientific understanding. There is now a difference: an accumulating body of evidence not only confirms the Government's concern, but suggests that whatever these phenomena are, they are not merely physical. They are aware.

ORIGINAL: In the preceding chapter, thirteen insiders and whistleblowers provided compelling testimony that the U.S. Government knows more than it has been saying about these phenomena. It is now clear that The Debunker's Playbook has successfully suppressed much of what is known about this phenomenon from the public eye. The same secrecy that kept this from American citizens has also impeded a full understanding of what is really happening in our skies and our oceans. Now, there is a difference: an accumulating body of evidence not only confirms the Government's concern over UAP, but it also suggests that whatever these phenomena are, they are not merely physical. They are aware.

PASTE TO FIND → In the preceding chapter, thirteen insiders and whistleblowers provided compelling testimony

dialogue.text: DONE v353: Applied to manuscript.

items.text: [L-07 — LOGIC] Ch11 p99: Rewrite VASCO TextOpen — bury administrative setup, open with animating idea.

dialogues.text:
PROPOSED: Astronomer Beatriz Villarroel found evidence of UAP by looking at stars that weren't there anymore. The Vanishing and Appearing Sources during a Century of Observations (VASCO) project analyzed digitized photographic plates from the Palomar Observatory Sky Survey: 2,718 observation days between November 1949 and April 1957, each plate a 50-minute exposure providing continuous coverage of the same field of sky.

ORIGINAL: Astronomer Beatriz Villarroel found evidence of UAP in an unexpected way. She was leading a study of the photographic plates from the Palomar Observatory Sky Survey (POSS-I), the Vanishing and Appearing Sources during a Century of Observations (VASCO) project. (Scientific Reports in October 2025.) It analyzed the digitized copies of the photographic plates taken of the sky over 2,718 observation days between November 19, 1949 and April 28, 1957. Each plate was a long exposure, typically lasting 50 minutes. Collectively, the photographs provided continuous coverage of the same field of sky for each of those observation days.

PASTE TO FIND → Astronomer Beatriz Villarroel found evidence of UAP in an unexpected way

dialogue.text: DONE v353: Applied to manuscript.

PROPOSED: This chapter surveys the supporting evidence: declassified documents, formal studies from multiple countries, independent peer-reviewed analyses, and case-specific material, beginning with the astronomical record.

ORIGINAL: This chapter introduces the supporting evidence including declassified documents, multiple countries' studies, independent peer-reviewed analyses, and specific cases. This chapter surveys the growing body of evidence for each proposition, beginning with the astronomical evidence.

PASTE TO FIND → This chapter introduces the supporting evidence including declassified documents

items.text: [L-09 — FLOW] Ch11 p134: Replace chapter-announcing close — 'The next chapter takes up…' undercuts the argument just established.

dialogues.text:
PROPOSED: Taken together, the declassified records, official investigations, scientific papers, material analyses, and pilot encounters build a consistent picture across seven decades and multiple countries. A small fraction of UAP cases with sufficient data still cannot be explained prosaically. The objects display performance that human technology cannot replicate, and they appear to be doing so intentionally. The question that follows is not whether they have a purpose. It is what that purpose is.

ORIGINAL: Taken together, the declassified records, official investigations, scientific papers, material analyses, and pilot encounters build a consistent picture across seven decades and multiple countries. A small fraction of UAP cases with sufficient data still cannot be explained prosaically. The objects display performance that human technology cannot replicate, and they appear to be doing so intentionally. The next chapter takes up what those 'intentions' might mean.

PASTE TO FIND → A small fraction of UAP cases with sufficient data still cannot be explained prosaically

dialogues.text:
PROPOSED: The assumption that observed behavior maps reliably to intent is itself a form of anthropocentrism — the same limits that apply to any intelligence attempting to read another. The first and most heavily documented pattern is surveillance of military capability.

dialogues.text:
PROPOSED: The craft appear intelligently controlled. The encounter pattern is global and consistent. Whatever is operating these objects remains unidentified. How they can be here at all is the next question — and no one outside classified programs knows the answer. The hypotheses that follow are tools for analysis, not conclusions. The Galileo Project, the Sol Foundation, and the Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies are working to build the evidence base that could elevate them. Until that record is public, they remain what they are: possible explanations.

ORIGINAL: The previous chapter established what the behavioral and experiencer records report: the craft appear intelligently controlled, the encounter pattern is global and consistent, and whatever is operating these objects remains unidentified. This chapter takes the next step, asking how they can be here at all. No one outside classified programs knows the answer with certainty. The hypotheses that follow are presented as tools for analysis, not as conclusions. The evidence required to elevate any of them to a verdict has not yet been made public, though ongoing efforts, among them the Galileo Project, the Sol Foundation, and the Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies, are working to change that. Until they do, the hypotheses remain what they are: possible explanations.

PASTE TO FIND → The previous chapter established what the behavioral and experiencer records report

items.text:
End Notes — construct standalone section after Conclusion

Current state: chapter notes exist as embedded ‘Chapter notes’ sub-sections (bheading2 style) within individual chapter bodies throughout the manuscript. There is no standalone End Notes section.

Decision (2026-06-20): End Notes will be a standalone unnumbered section placed immediately after the Conclusion and before the appendices. No appendix label. Same treatment as Further Reading and Bibliography.

Final back matter sequence (print):
Conclusion → End Notes → A1–A15 → Further Reading → Bibliography → Glossary → Index → Acknowledgements → About the Author

Ebook variant (no Index):
Conclusion → End Notes → A1–A15 → Further Reading → Bibliography → Glossary → Acknowledgements → About the Author

Work required:
(1) MASTER: Extract all embedded ‘Chapter notes’ sub-sections from chapter bodies and consolidate into a new standalone End Notes section immediately after the Conclusion. Each chapter’s notes become a named sub-section (e.g., ‘Chapter 1 notes’) under a single BOOKCHAPTER heading: ‘End Notes’.
(2) Body cross-references: In-chapter callouts to chapter notes resolve correctly — content doesn’t change, only location.
(3) Footnote navigation: Bidirectional ref_/note_ bookmarks travel with the note paragraphs. Four-count gate must run after move to confirm no links broke.
(4) TOC: End Notes must appear in TOC between Conclusion and Appendices.
(5) Ebook: i_ebook_backmatter_sequence captures ebook placement. Update that item once construction is complete.

SEQUENCING: Run after prose is locked and all A-passes are confirmed clean. Must run BEFORE B4 Footnote Pass — the four-count gate after the move is the B4 prerequisite check. Do not combine with an active prose pass session.

items.content:
UAP/UFO Daily Intelligence Report — v5
Saturday, June 20, 2026 · First run

DATE & SKY EVENT CONFIRMATION
Date: Saturday, June 20, 2026. No active meteor showers. No SpaceX launches confirmed today. No exceptional ISS passes flagged. No sky event credibility concerns.

1. SIGHTINGS
Sources: Americans for Safe Aerospace (safeaerospace.org). NUFORC not searched (removed from v5 prompt).

Three recent ASA pilot reports meeting credibility criteria (dates unspecified on public page):

• Gulfstream V / Van Nuys approach — Pilot and copilot at 51,000 ft observed multiple glowing orbs launching from ground, ascending above their altitude, disappearing into space. Separate orb hovered above cloud layers during Van Nuys approach. Video captured on pilot's iPhone. Credentialed observers, video media. [Americans for Safe Aerospace — Pilot Reports](https://www.safeaerospace.org/reports)

• Northwest of Sitka, Alaska — Airline pilot observed metallic, disc-shaped object with telescopic/segmented appendage beneath it. Credentialed observer. [Americans for Safe Aerospace — Pilot Reports](https://www.safeaerospace.org/reports)

• Hawaii / orbs over water — Schoolteacher and second witness recorded 10+ minutes of orbs flying over water and interacting; one reportedly approached within yards of witnesses. Objects displayed instantaneous acceleration prior to recording. Multiple witnesses, video media. [Americans for Safe Aerospace — Pilot Reports](https://www.safeaerospace.org/reports)

No sky events active that would suggest misidentification for any of the above.

2. NEWS & ANNOUNCEMENTS

UAP Science Advisory Council established (June 12–18, 2026): White House, AARO, ODNI, FBI, and Intelligence Community tasked Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb to assemble and lead a new UAP Science Advisory Council. Rear Admiral Tim Gallaudet (ret.) confirmed as member, signaling transmedium/undersea scope. Stated aim: build better sensors and AI tools for Pentagon. Significant concern: Loeb confirmed June 16 his team holds no access to classified information and no legal authority — unlike NASA's FACA-chartered UAP study, this council has no official public record. Disclosure advocates have flagged it as a potential credibility fig leaf. [DefenseScoop, June 17, 2026](https://defensescoop.com/2026/06/17/new-science-advisory-council-forms-to-help-us-government-resolve-the-uap-mystery/) · [Skeptic.com, June 15, 2026](https://www.skeptic.com/article/michael-shermer-named-to-white-house-uap-science-advisory-council/)

PURSUE Release 3 (June 12, 2026): Department of War published third batch — 53 documents, 10 images from CIA, FBI, NASA, DoD; 6 videos; 3 NASA audio recordings. Centerpiece: June 5, 2026 AARO report signed by Director Jon Kosloski describing October 2023 law enforcement encounter with orange "mother" orb launching smaller red orbs; 40% of reported phenomena remain unresolved. Also includes: 2022 Fort Carson case (5 soldiers, potato-shaped opalescent object near Cheyenne Mountain); CIA historical materials confirming U-2/OXCART flights caused >50% of UFO reports in late 1950s–1960s; FBI FD-302 documenting 2024 northeastern U.S. orb sighting; Gordon Cooper/Walter Cronkite 1962 audio debrief. WAR.GOV/UFO has received 1.7 billion hits since May 8, 2026 launch. Fourth release in development. [CBS News, June 12, 2026](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ufo-files-pentagon-3rd-release-documents-videos/) · [NBC News, June 12, 2026](https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/national-security/third-batch-declassified-ufo-files-reveals-sightings-world-investigate-rcna349775) · [Department of War](https://www.war.gov/News/Releases/Release/Article/4515408/)

UAP Disclosure Foundation Forum — June 25, 2026 (upcoming): Washington D.C. forum to discuss national security, technology, religious, and philosophical implications of file releases. Jordan Flowers noted third release raises possibility China and Russia may have retrieved and attempted to reverse-engineer their own objects. [NewsNation, June 19, 2026](https://www.newsnationnow.com/space/ufo/ufo-experts-latest-uap-file-release/)

3. LEGISLATIVE & POLICY TRACKER

H.R. 1187 — UAP Transparency Act (active): Rep. Burchett's bill requires President to direct all federal agencies to declassify UAP records and post publicly. Referred to Committee on Oversight and Government Reform. [Congress.gov](https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/1187)

FY2026 NDAA — UAP provisions in force: Three mandates enacted — AARO expanded congressional briefings; Pentagon must brief lawmakers on NORAD/NORTHCOM UAP intercepts since 2004; AARO must account for UAP security classification guides. [DefenseScoop, December 10, 2025](https://defensescoop.com/2025/12/10/uap-ufo-military-intercepts-north-america-fy-2026-ndaa/)

No new bills or hearing announcements confirmed June 14–20. Congress.gov noted data issues as of June 16.

4. BOOKS & PERIODICALS
30-day window: May 21 – June 20, 2026

No new peer-reviewed papers or nonfiction books confirmed within the 30-day window.

Notable recent (outside window): "UAP Operational Presence, 1945–1975" — Ian M. Porritt, Larry J. Hancock, Sean Grosvenor · SCU · April 20, 2026. Fourth and synthesizing paper in SCU's multi-year series. Uses Project Blue Book Sparks database and NICAP archives. Conclusion: UAP activity consistent with small, mobile reconnaissance force under resource constraints, with persistent focus on U.S. nuclear infrastructure. [SCU](https://www.explorescu.org/post/scu-publishes-comprehensive-analysis-of-uap-operational-presence-1945-1975) · [Zenodo open access](https://zenodo.org/records/16299623)

Forthcoming: SCU 2026 Conference, Toronto, July 24–26. Keynote: Christopher Mellon. Speaker: Dr. Randy Bostick (former AARO science advisor). [explorescu.org](https://explorescu.org)

5. EXPLAINED & RESOLVED CASES

PURSUE Release 3 CIA materials explicitly confirm U-2 and OXCART spy flights caused more than half of all UFO reports in late 1950s and most of the 1960s; Project Blue Book gave the public false explanations to protect the programs. Significant historical resolved-case disclosure in the government's own words. [CBS News, June 12, 2026](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ufo-files-pentagon-3rd-release-documents-videos/)

No current-window sightings with confirmed mundane explanations identified.

6. CARRY-OVER ITEMS

First run. No carry-over items.

7. SOURCE ACCESS LOG
✅ war.gov/UFO — PURSUE portal (via official press releases)
✅ CBS News, NBC News, NewsNation, DefenseScoop — confirmed via search
✅ Americans for Safe Aerospace (safeaerospace.org) — pilot reports page accessible
✅ Congress.gov — H.R. 1187 accessible; site noted data issues as of June 16
✅ SCU (explorescu.org) — April 2026 paper confirmed
✅ Disclosure Foundation (disclosure.org) — coverage confirmed
✅ Skeptic.com — Shermer appointment confirmed
⚠️ ASA pilot reports — accessible but individual reports lack precise dates
❌ AARO (aaro.mil) — not fetched directly; surfaced via secondary sources only
❌ Sol Foundation — no new 30-day content confirmed; not directly fetched

App production — platform keyboard detection

When hub is packaged as an app, detect Mac vs PC on first launch and swap all shortcut labels accordingly: Mac shows Cmd (⌘), PC shows Ctrl. Implementation options: (A) navigator.platform / userAgentData detection on load, store preference in localStorage, render shortcut labels dynamically. (B) One-time setup screen on first launch — "Are you on a Mac or PC?" — with option to change later in Settings > General. Option B preferred: explicit, user-controlled, works even when detection is ambiguous (e.g. iPad with keyboard). Settings > General would get a "Platform" toggle: Mac / PC. All shortcut table cells and tooltip text render from a platform-aware helper function rather than hardcoded strings. Deferred until app packaging phase.

dialogue.text: Flagged 2026-06-20: When packaged as app, detect Mac vs PC and swap Cmd/Ctrl labels throughout shortcut UI. Recommendation: explicit one-time setup question on first launch + Settings > General toggle. Deferred to app packaging phase.

road.note: Full prose pass across all chapters. Trigger: "Prose pass [scope]". Covers: A1 Word-by-word, A2 Spelling/grammar, A3 AI-speak, A4 Paragraph logic, A5 Paragraph structure, A6 Flow check, A7 Thematic echo, A8 Sterling commercial read. Run before any Tier B work on the same scope.

road.label: Tier B — Mechanical Pass
road.note: Mechanical verification passes. Run after Tier A is confirmed clean. Each sub-pass is discrete and logs on completion.

road.label: B1 — Formatting Pass
road.note: Style ID compliance, image placement rule, caption construction, section breaks, pull quote/Attribution pairs, TextOpen after subheadings, chapter structure order, two-space title normalization.

road.label: B2 — Citation Pass
road.note: Four-point check: (1) every significant factual claim has a marker; (2) all citations are primary sources per hierarchy; (3) each citation fetched and read to confirm it supports the specific claim; (4) every citation appears in A15 Bibliography with complete entry. Logs to Citation_Verification_Log.md.

road.label: B3 — Cross-Reference Pass
road.note: Every "see Chapter N" and "Appendix AX" reference verified to resolve to real content with matching material. Back matter included. Logs to CrossRef_Verification_Log.md.

road.label: B4 — Footnote Pass
road.note: Four-count gate: ref bookmarks = note bookmarks = forward hyperlinks = return hyperlinks. Adjacent marker audit. Sequence integrity 1→N. afootnote construction check. No orphaned markers.

road.label: B5 — Appendix Pass
road.note: A2 sightings scan, A12 figures/diagrams, A15 Quotations location codes, A15 Bibliography new entries, Glossary new terms, Index new subjects. Run after B2 confirmed for relevant chapters.

road.label: B6 — URL Pass
road.note: Every hyperlink verified to resolve. Invisible-hyperlink rendering confirmed (color=auto, u=none, no Hyperlink style).

road.label: B7 — Index Pass
road.note: Run last — after all content is locked. Locator codes, bookmark naming, subentry formatting, print vs. Kindle locator styles, exclusion rules (A15, A12). Editing body content after B7 potentially invalidates locators.

road.label: Appendix sequence change A2, A12
road.note: Appendix sequence rationalization: consolidate reference/lookup appendices (A2, A12) into final grouping per filed remap doc requires a full cross-reference cascade pass.

road.label: Tier C — Production Pass
road.note: Final production passes. Run only after Tier A and Tier B are confirmed clean for all content. C0 precedes C1 and C2.

road.note: One full read for anything missed. Minor prose only — no structural changes. Sterling final commercial assessment.

road.note: Heading hierarchy, TOC integrity, front/back matter, metadata, cross-reference integrity, image resolution (72–150 DPI), table simplification, line break removal, hyperlink audit. Status: Done v230.

road.label: C2 — Production Pass Print
road.note: Image resolution (300 DPI), color/grayscale audit, margin/gutter (6″×9″, 1″ margins), page breaks on recto, widows/orphans, index locator style (sz=16 print override), print index completeness.

road.label: Flow — continuity and rhythm read

road.note: Any unsourced factual claims surfaced by fresh read?

road.label: Agent/Publisher Decision and Pitch
road.note: 60-day pitch window to PublicAffairs, Dutton, Twelve, and one other. Lead with June 9 and Turner document. Differentiation: analytical/structural book vs. Elizondo memoir. Hard deadline: if nothing compelling by response window close, proceed with self-publishing. Book must be pitch-ready before family vacation June 30.

road.note: Decide whether to pitch and finalize list of 3–4 publishers (PublicAffairs, Dutton, Twelve + one). Make this call before prose is locked.

road.label: Build pitch package
road.note: Query letter, 2-page synopsis, sample chapters (suggest Ch. 1 + Ch. 9 or Ch. 16). Lead with June 9 Capitol steps and Turner document as differentiation hook.

road.label: Send to publishers — pitch window opens
road.note: Submit to all targets simultaneously. Log submission dates.

road.label: Publisher response window
road.note: Allow 60 days from submission. Follow up at 30 days if no acknowledgment.

road.label: Agent Pitch

road.label: Response window closes — proceed to self-publish if no compelling offer
Update over-classified.com book description
road.note: Reflect June 9 Capitol press conference, Turner/NAA document, Aldo Rebelo/Varginha confirmation, PURSUE release milestone, and current disclosure momentum. Must be live before publisher pitch submissions go out.

road.note: Query literary agents before or alongside publisher pitch. All three target publishers (PublicAffairs, Dutton/Caliber, Twelve) require agent submission. Top targets: Keith Urbahn (Javelin), Eric Lupfer (Calligraph), Matt Latimer (Javelin). 60-day query window. If agent secured before vacation, they handle publisher submissions. If no agent by Aug 29, proceed direct self-publish.

road.label: Agent decision — go/no-go on querying
road.note: Decide whether to pursue agents simultaneously with or before publisher pitch. Agents can submit to publishers more effectively and negotiate better terms. Time cost: 2–4 weeks to query; 4–8 weeks typical response.

road.label: Build query letter + 25-page sample
road.note: Query letter: 1 page. Hook (June 9 + Turner/NAA document), premise (analytical/structural vs. memoir wave), author credentials, chapter outline, word count. Sample: Ch.1 + Ch.5 opener. Calligraph: paste 25 pages in email body, no attachments, submissions@calligraphlit.com, name agent in subject. Javelin: check javelindc.com for current query instructions.

road.label: Query Urbahn + Lupfer + Latimer simultaneously
road.note: All three can be queried at once — no exclusivity requirement at query stage. Urbahn/Latimer (Javelin): national security insider nonfiction lane. Lupfer (Calligraph): explicitly lists national security and defense as focus; fastest known response time (1 day on record). Lead pitch: differentiation from Elizondo memoir wave + June 9 Capitol event + Turner/NAA document.

road.label: Agent response window
road.note: Allow 6–8 weeks from submission. Follow up at 4 weeks if no acknowledgment. If agent offers representation, evaluate deal terms before committing.

Update EIN number Record with new Vigilia Name

Move Hub to Vercel
road.note: Fix conflict between over-classified.com (Vercel) and hub (GitHub Pages) by moving hub to Vercel. Four steps — takes about 30 minutes total. No coding required.

road.label: Step 1 — Create Vercel account & import oc-hub repo
road.note: Go to vercel.com → Sign Up → Continue with GitHub → Import the brownd11-a11y/oc-hub repository. No settings to change — Vercel detects it is a static site automatically. Click Deploy.

road.label: Step 2 — Assign hub.over-classified.com in Vercel
road.note: In your new Vercel project → Settings → Domains → Add: hub.over-classified.com. Vercel will show you a CNAME value to copy (looks like: cname.vercel-dns.com). Leave this tab open.

road.label: Step 3 — Add CNAME at your DNS registrar
road.note: Log in to wherever you bought over-classified.com → DNS settings → Add record: Type=CNAME, Name=hub, Value=paste what Vercel gave you in Step 2. Save. Takes up to 1 hour to propagate.

road.label: Step 4 — Remove custom domain from GitHub Pages
road.note: Go to github.com → brownd11-a11y/oc-hub → Settings → Pages → Custom domain → clear the field and Save. Hub push workflow unchanged — Claude still pushes index.html; Vercel redeploys automatically.

road.label: W3.1 — Mechanical pass rescan
road.note: Re-run against chapters edited since v229.

road.label: W3.8 — Word-by-word rescan
road.note: Focus on recently touched sections.

road.note: Running heads · widow/orphan · image resolution · page breaks · TOC numbers.

road.note: Refresh all PURSUE references in manuscript body to reflect latest counts of file drops and media coverage at time of edit.

road.label: Word for Word on entire book
Thematic echo scan
road.note: Flag arguments or phrases appearing in substantially similar form across chapters.

road.label: No material repetition — Dave reviews and approves any cuts
Export print file for test run

outreach.name: Eric Burlison
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Missouri 7th — UAP Caucus / Task Force

notes.text: 1108 Longworth HOB, Washington DC 20515. (202) 225-6536. Most active House UAP Caucus member on legislation and dark-money financing of programs. Co-sponsored UAP Transparency Act 2024. Introduced bill to abolish AARO Apr 2026. June 9 Capitol press conference speaker. Approach: lead with the constitutional accountability framing — his language. Mention the F-117/NDA closing argument in Ch.6 directly.

outreach.name: Tim Burchett
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Tennessee 2nd — UAP oversight, DOGE subcommittee chair

notes.text: 1110 Longworth HOB, Washington DC 20515. Web form requires district address verification — non-constituents should write direct mail or use press contact. Long-standing UAP advocate, first major House hearing organizer. June 9 Capitol press conference speaker. Approach: plain-spoken directness matches his style — lead with "the Government has known and won't say" framing, not academic language.

outreach.name: Anna Paulina Luna
outreach.role: U.S. Rep., Florida 13th — Chair, Task Force on Declassification

notes.text: 226 Cannon HOB, Washington DC 20515. Press contact: David Leatherwood — David.Leatherwood@mail.house.gov. First Mexican-American woman from Florida, Air Force veteran, chairs Task Force on Declassification of Federal Secrets. June 9 immunity announcement speaker — quoted in book. Approach: lead with the immunity/whistleblower angle from Ch.18 and Conclusion. Her own words are in A15.

outreach.name: Lue Elizondo
outreach.role: Fmr. AATIP director / NYT bestselling author (Imminent)

notes.text: Contact via official site form. Also active on X @LueElizondo. His book Imminent (HarperCollins, Aug 2024) is the dominant insider memoir in this space — NYT bestseller. This book is the analytical/structural complement to his memoir, not competition. Approach: differentiation is key — position as the book that explains the system Elizondo navigated, not another insider account. Mention Ch.9 Manufacturing Doubt and the Grusch smear parallel directly. FLAG: retrieve Elizondo past-chat context before drafting outreach.

outreach.name: Christopher Mellon
outreach.role: Fmr. Dep. Asst. Sec. Def. for Intelligence / UAP advocate

notes.text: Website: christophermellon.net. Active on X. Keynote speaker at 2026 SCU Conference (Toronto, July 24-26). Called AARO Volume I "the most error-ridden and unsatisfactory government report I can recall reading." Strongly critical of AARO — directly aligned with book's Ch.6 and Ch.10 arguments. Approach: lead with the AARO failure analysis and the classification architecture argument. He will recognize the structural argument immediately.

outreach.name: Ross Coulthart
outreach.role: Investigative journalist — NewsNation Reality Check / author

notes.text: Website: rosscoulthart.com. X: @rosscoulthart. NewsNation special correspondent hosting Reality Check. Broke the Grusch story in June 2023 exclusive. Five-time Walkley Award winner. Likely receptive — this book supports the accountability argument his journalism makes. Approach: emphasize the primary-source architecture and the Turner/NAA Australian document, which connects to his investigative interests.

outreach.name: Kevin Knuth
outreach.role: Prof. of Physics, Univ. at Albany — UAPx VP Science & Technology

notes.text: Email: kknuth@albany.edu. Phone: (518) 442-4501. Knuth Information Physics Lab, Physics 228, University at Albany, 1400 Washington Ave, Albany NY 12222. Co-author of landmark Entropy paper on UAP flight characteristics (cited in Ch.13). VP Science & Technology at UAPx — co-founded with USS Princeton veterans. Approach: lead with the Entropy paper citation and how the book frames his work within the broader institutional response to scientific UAP research.

outreach.name: Avi Loeb
outreach.role: Harvard Prof. of Science — Galileo Project founder

notes.text: Email: aloeb@cfa.harvard.edu. Staff assistant Uma Mirani: (617) 496-5870. Center for Astrophysics, 60 Garden Street MS-51, Cambridge MA 02138. Frank B. Baird Jr. Professor of Science. NYT/WSJ bestselling author (Extraterrestrial, Interstellar). Top 3 astronomer worldwide by publication impact (ScholarGPS 2024). Top-5 TED talk 2024. Approach: he has been explicit that science must take UAP seriously — frame this book as the institutional accountability argument that explains why the scientific community was shut out for 80 years.

outreach.name: PublicAffairs Books
outreach.role: Publisher — Hachette / serious nonfiction, current affairs, public policy

notes.text: Publisher: Clive Priddle. Part of Hachette Book Group. Publicity: PEmail@hbgusa.com. Backlist: Surveillance Capitalism (Zuboff), Rise of the Warrior Cop (Balko), The Jakarta Method. AGENT REQUIRED — no unsolicited submissions. Perfect fit: serious nonfiction about government accountability and institutional failure. Differentiation pitch: analytical/structural book vs. Elizondo memoir. Lead with June 9 and Turner/NAA document. Hachette HQ: 1290 Avenue of the Americas, New York NY 10104.

outreach.name: Dutton Caliber
outreach.role: Publisher — Penguin Random House / military, national security, intelligence

notes.text: Publisher: John Parsley (VP/Editorial Director). Caliber is Dutton's military/national security imprint — ideal fit. Boutique list ~40 books/year. AGENT REQUIRED. Backlist: numerous NYT bestsellers in national security/military nonfiction. Approach: pitch as a serious national security accountability book, not a UAP book. The Caliber imprint specifically targets this audience. Parsley edits narrative nonfiction; Pilar Garcia-Brown handles "deeply researched stories that engage with contemporary issues."

outreach.name: Twelve Books
outreach.role: Publisher — Hachette / singular books, unique perspectives

notes.text: Part of Grand Central Publishing / Hachette. Publishes only 12 titles per year — extremely selective. AGENT REQUIRED. Backlist: Tammy Duckworth, Condoleezza Rice, Dana Perino, Maureen Dowd. Mission: "singular books by authors with unique perspectives and compelling authority." This book fits the mission statement precisely. Lead pitch: the analytical rigor differentiates it from the memoir wave. Approach through agent only.

outreach.name: Keith Urbahn — Javelin
outreach.role: Literary agent — national security, political nonfiction, D.C. insider books

notes.text: Javelin DC, Old Town Alexandria VA. urbahn@javelindc.com (verify current). Co-founder with Matt Latimer. Former Rumsfeld chief of staff, Yale, U.S. Navy reserve intelligence officer. Clients: James Comey, Tucker Carlson, Don Rumsfeld. BEST FIT: national security + D.C. insider nonfiction is his exact lane. He has cleared national security review himself. Approach: lead with the structural/analytical differentiation from the Elizondo memoir wave. Mention June 9 Capitol event and the Turner/NAA document as the hook. Query first — 1-2 page letter + brief sample.

outreach.name: Eric Lupfer — Calligraph
outreach.role: Literary agent — national security, defense, intelligence, history

notes.text: Calligraph Literary Agency, New York & Boston. Submissions: submissions@calligraphlit.com — include agent name in subject line, query + 25 pages in body (no attachments). Formerly WME (14 years), Fletcher & Co, UTA. Moved to Calligraph 2025. Explicitly lists "national security and defense" and "military, intelligence, and national defense professionals" as focus areas. Army brat background. Fastest query response on record (1 day turnaround per QueryTracker). STRONG FIT. Approach: query emphasizes primary-source rigor, classification architecture argument, and the June 9 Capitol event.

outreach.name: Matt Latimer — Javelin
outreach.role: Literary agent — political nonfiction, White House/Pentagon books

notes.text: Javelin DC — co-founder with Keith Urbahn. Former Bush White House speechwriter, Rumsfeld chief speechwriter, Columbia journalism MA, Michigan Law JD. Clients: political figures, historians, journalists. Could query Urbahn or Latimer at Javelin — both have national security background. Query both simultaneously or choose one. Approach same as Urbahn query.
